behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names the target file, function, data structure type, error codes (ZBADVERSION/ZNONODE), log format strings, and counter format expected by tests.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md specifies exact types ('std::vector<std::pair<String, int32_t>>'), exact log strings ('Removing stale processing node: {}', 'Removed {}/{} stale processing nodes'), and exact error codes to handle — essentially dictating the implementation rather than describing broken behavior.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests accept flexible variable names, both emplace_back/push_back, and either .first/.second or structured bindings, though they do require the specific pair type and log format which the instruction mandates.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All tests use regex pattern matching on the source file content (read_target_file + re.search); none compile, execute, or invoke the code.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require multiple coordinated patterns (pair type, tryRemove with 2 args, ZBADVERSION handling, counter increment, specific log formats) which a stub cannot satisfy.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each required behavior (version storage, tryRemove with version, ZBADVERSION handling, trace logs, counter tracking) has a corresponding test function.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are separated into well-named functions each with docstrings describing what behavior they verify.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code modification, not structured data production.

no_typos.reason: File paths, function names, and identifiers in instruction.md match the source file and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names the exact file 'src/Storages/ObjectStorageQueue/ObjectStorageQueueMetadata.cpp' and exact function 'cleanupPersistentProcessingNodes()', removing any localization effort.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, no config edits involved.
